## 🌎 Geopolitics

### Putin and Xi Declare "Highest Level in History" Ties; Power of Siberia 2 Pipeline Deal Eludes Beijing

Russian President Vladimir Putin and Chinese leader Xi Jinping met in Beijing on May 20, 2026, where they announced their strategic partnership had reached "the highest level in history." Russia's oil exports to China grew 35% in Q1 2026, with the two countries advancing discussions on the Power of Siberia 2 pipeline — a critical infrastructure project to redirect Russian natural gas from Europe toward Asia. However, Beijing stopped short of finalizing the deal, signaling continued wariness over energy dependence on a single supplier.

**Why it matters.** This deepening Russia-China axis directly shapes global energy flows, commodity pricing, and your operational landscape. As a consulting practitioner in India's market, you're watching two things: first, the structural shift in energy geopolitics that creates arbitrage and hedging opportunities (Russian gas via Asia, LNG spot price volatility, rupee exposure to commodity swings). Second, China's hesitant stance — preferring diversified suppliers over Russian dependency — mirrors India's own hedging strategy on Russian oil and signals how fragile energy-driven alliances remain under pressure. The "multipolar world" they're building affects how India positions itself between the US, Russia, and China.

**Action.** Monitor oil futures and LNG spreads for the next 30 days; Chinese cautionism typically precedes broader commodity corrections. Read Chatham House's latest on China-Russia ties (linked) to understand the limits of this alliance — critical context for long-duration prop bets on geopolitical risk premia. Track whether Power of Siberia 2 financing moves by June; delays suggest Beijing is constraining Moscow's leverage elsewhere.

*Sources: [pbs.org](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/putin-and-xi-hail-their-friendship-and-growing-energy-trade-at-their-meeting-in-beijing) · [moderndiplomacy.eu](https://moderndiplomacy.eu/2026/05/20/xi-and-putin-strengthen-china-russia-partnership-with-energy-and-trade-in-focus/) · [chathamhouse.org](https://www.chathamhouse.org/2026/05/china-and-russias-strategic-duo-endures-its-limits-are-clear)*

---

### Trump's Russia Oil Sanctions Waiver for India Expires; Tariff Fate Unclear as Energy Trade Friction Remains

The Trump administration's temporary exemption for India's purchase of Russian oil — which lifted the previous 25% tariff surcharge imposed in August 2025 — has expired with no formal extension announced as of May 26. Washington has instructed U.S. agencies to monitor Indian imports closely and will decide whether to reimpose duties if New Delhi increases Russian oil volumes. This creates acute uncertainty for Indian refiners, who have scaled Russian crude purchases to 2+ million barrels per day and now face potential tariff snapback.

**Why it matters.** This is your direct exposure. India's energy independence increasingly depends on Russian supply at competitive prices — tariff whipsaws directly compress refiner margins and flow through to downstream inflation and rupee volatility. For your consulting work, this is a lever: tariff risk feeds into corporate hedging demand, credit spreads on refiners widen, and policy uncertainty becomes a pricing factor in India's energy-intensive sectors (cement, steel, chemicals). Simultaneously, External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar has been explicitly messaging against sanctions weaponization at BRICS forums, signaling New Delhi will absorb some tariff cost rather than abandon Russian trade — valuable context for macro positioning on India's growth and policy resilience.

**Action.** Watch tariff announcements weekly through June; set a monitor on US agency commentary about India-Russia oil flows. Read Carnegie Endowment's latest on US sanctions and India's Russian imports (linked) to quantify exposure. Flag refiners' Q2 earnings calls for management guidance on tariff contingencies — this tells you how badly the market is pricing the risk.

*Sources: [geopoliticalmonitor.com](https://www.geopoliticalmonitor.com/tariffs-as-sanction-enforcement-in-the-us-india-deal/) · [carnegieendowment.org](https://carnegieendowment.org/posts/2025/11/the-impact-of-us-sanctions-and-tariffs-on-indias-russian-oil-imports?lang=en) · [organiser.org](https://organiser.org/2026/05/14/353474/bharat/brics-india-2026-eam-jaishankar-slams-politics-of-sanctions-warns-of-west-asia-shockwaves-to-global-economy/amp/)*

## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview Found 10,000+ Zero-Days Autonomously; Glasswing Update Drops May 26

Anthropic published a Project Glasswing progress update on May 26 detailing that Claude Mythos Preview — an unrestricted internal model not available to the public — has autonomously identified over 10,000 high- and critical-severity zero-day vulnerabilities (previously unknown flaws with no existing patches) across every major operating system and web browser. One confirmed find is CVE-2026-4747, a 17-year-old remote code execution vulnerability in FreeBSD's NFS implementation granting full root access, which Mythos discovered and exploited without human guidance at each step. On standard benchmarks, Mythos Preview scores 94.6% on GPQA Diamond (PhD-level physics, biology, and chemistry questions that reliably stump domain experts), 93.9% on SWE-bench Verified, and 83.1% on CyberGym. The model remains restricted to Project Glasswing partners — Anthropic's coalition of OS vendors, browser developers, and open-source maintainers — with no general API access planned.

**Why it matters.** The capability trajectory here is qualitatively different from prior "AI finds bugs" announcements. Mythos is chaining vulnerabilities into exploit paths autonomously — discovery, triage, PoC generation, and attack-path construction without per-step human prompting. At 93.9% SWE-bench Verified, it has also crossed a threshold where agentic coding pipelines built today will need to be stress-tested against a model that can modify or subvert its own scaffolding. The restricted-access model creates a defender-first window, but it is explicitly time-bounded: Anthropic's framing is that they are releasing to defenders *before* similar capability becomes broadly available, which implies they expect it to become broadly available. For anyone building security tooling, red-team pipelines, or software supply-chain integrity systems, the architecture assumptions underlying "human reviews AI output" are now the ones to revisit.

**Action.** Read the Project Glasswing technical disclosure at red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview and the anthropic.com/glasswing partner page. If you are building agentic systems that touch production code or infrastructure, run SWE-bench Verified evals on your current scaffolding baseline now — the gap between Mythos (93.9%) and publicly accessible models is measurable and sets your threat model ceiling. If you are on a Glasswing-eligible project (major OS, browser, critical open-source infrastructure), apply for partner access directly through the Glasswing portal; the window before broader model release is the leverage point.

*Sources: [helpnetsecurity.com](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/05/26/anthropic-project-glasswing-update/) · [red.anthropic.com](https://red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview/) · [anthropic.com](https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing)*

---

### Gemini 3.5 Flash Beats Its Own Pro Tier on Agent Benchmarks at Fraction of the Cost

Google shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O on May 19, and it has since become the default model behind the Gemini app and Google Search's AI Mode. On Terminal-Bench 2.1 — a benchmark measuring autonomous coding-agent performance in shell environments — it scores 76.2%, outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on the same suite. It scores 83.6% on MCP Atlas (multi-step tool-use reliability across Model Context Protocol endpoints), 84.2% on CharXiv Reasoning (advanced chart and visual understanding), and holds a 1,656 Elo on GDPval-AA for real-world agentic task evaluation. The model has a 1M-token context window, 65k max output tokens, four selectable thinking levels with thought preservation across turns, and Google claims 4x token throughput versus comparable frontier models at its tier.

**Why it matters.** A flash-tier model (meaning a smaller, faster, cheaper model in a lab's lineup) beating the previous generation's pro tier on agentic benchmarks is an actual architectural signal, not marketing copy. It means the cost-performance tradeoff for building multi-step agent pipelines just shifted: you can now run Gemini 3.5 Flash at MCP Atlas 83.6% quality for what you previously paid for Gemini 3.1 Pro at lower tool-use reliability. The 1M-token context with thought preservation across turns is directly relevant for long-horizon agent tasks where accumulated reasoning state matters — unlike stateless prompting, the model can maintain working memory (its internal reasoning trace) across a multi-turn tool-calling loop. For anyone pricing out agentic infrastructure, Flash's throughput advantage also means lower latency on parallel agent fanouts, which is where cost compounds in production.

**Action.** Pull the Gemini 3.5 Flash model card from deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-5-flash and check MCP Atlas 83.6% versus whatever model your current agent stack uses — that number is the clearest apples-to-apples on tool-use reliability. If you have a workload currently running on Gemini 3.1 Pro, run an A/B on 3.5 Flash immediately: the throughput gain alone may justify the switch before you even benchmark accuracy. Gemini 3.5 Pro is in testing and expected next month; hold off on long-term architecture lock-in for the highest-complexity reasoning tasks until that release lands.

*Sources: [blog.google](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/gemini-models/gemini-3-5/) · [deepmind.google](https://deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-5-flash/) · [marktechpost.com](https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/05/20/google-introduces-gemini-3-5-flash-at-i-o-2026-a-faster-and-cheaper-model-for-ai-agents-and-coding/)*

## 🇮🇳 India

### Piper Serica Launches ₹800 Crore Bharat Tech Fund for Deeptech and AI Startups

Piper Serica, a Mumbai-based asset manager, has established a Category II Alternative Investment Fund with ₹800 crore corpus to invest in semiconductor, AI, space-tech, defence, fintech, and biosciences startups. The fund will deploy capital across approximately 22 companies in Series A and B rounds, writing cheques of ₹25–40 crore per company, with a target gross IRR of 30 percent and a six-year holding period. Fund closure is expected by December 2026. This follows Piper Serica's first fund, a ₹273-crore Category I AIF, which has deployed 85 percent and already backed companies including Alt Mobility, Sensesemi, and Freed.

**Why it matters.** This dedicated fund directly de-risks early-stage deeptech and AI builders in India. If you're a research engineer considering founding a semiconductor, AI, or space-tech startup in India, or evaluating which funds back founders in your domain, Piper Serica's size and sector focus (especially the AI emphasis) signals patient capital willing to write the right-sized rounds for capital-intensive deeptech. The six-year hold period aligns with deeptech timelines where regulatory approvals and manufacturing scale take years.

**Action.** Track Piper Serica's investment thesis document on their website for focus areas within AI and semiconductors. Cross-reference their portfolio (Alt Mobility, Sensesemi, Pantherun) to understand stage and pace. If building deeptech, prepare a pitch deck emphasizing proprietary IP—the fund explicitly targets IP-led founders. Bookmark the December 2026 closure date; earlier-stage companies should reach out within six months.

*Sources: [[Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/amp/industry/news/piper-serica-launches-800-crore-bharat-tech-fund-for-deep-tech-startups-126052000855_1.html)]([Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/amp/industry/news/piper-serica-launches-800-crore-bharat-tech-fund-for-deep-tech-startups-126052000855_1.html)) · [[YourStory](https://yourstory.com/2026/05/piper-serica-launches-rs-800-crore-bharat-tech-fund)]([YourStory](https://yourstory.com/2026/05/piper-serica-launches-rs-800-crore-bharat-tech-fund)) · [[Indian Startup Times](https://www.indianstartuptimes.com/news/piper-serica-launches-inr-800-crore-bharat-tech-fund-to-back-indias-deeptech-and-ip-led-startups/)]([Indian Startup Times](https://www.indianstartuptimes.com/news/piper-serica-launches-inr-800-crore-bharat-tech-fund-to-back-indias-deeptech-and-ip-led-startups/))*

### SEBI Clears Zepto's ₹11,000 Crore IPO, Largest Consumer-Tech Offering in Indian Markets

SEBI formally cleared Zepto's maiden public offering of ₹11,000 crore in early May 2026, making it one of India's largest consumer-tech IPOs. Founded in 2021 by Aadit Palicha and Kaivalya Vohra, the quick-commerce unicorn operates over 1,100 dark stores delivering groceries in under 10 minutes. The IPO combines fresh issue and secondary shares from existing investors including Bain Capital. Axis Bank, Motilal Oswal, Morgan Stanley, HSBC, and Goldman Sachs are advising the offering, with listing targeted for July–September 2026.

**Why it matters.** Zepto's SEBI clearance validates a 2021-founded, consumer-facing software business can scale to unicorn status and public markets within five years—a critical data point if you're building an AI or fintech product targeting India's consumer internet. The deal structure (OFS component from Bain Capital) signals how growth equity and VC exits work in Indian markets. For engineers evaluating when to join an early-stage startup versus a scaling company, this IPO proves the prize exists; for those already in a Series B or C company, it's a benchmark for unit economics and go-to-market timelines.

**Action.** File a DRHP (Detailed Red Herring Prospectus) watch alert once Zepto's public filing drops in June; the financial disclosures will show unit economics, CAC, and retention metrics relevant to any consumer-facing startup. Study the IPO prospectus when available to understand how Zepto justified ₹11,000 crore valuation—this is a template for exits. For founders, analyze pricing band and anchor investor demand; it signals appetite for India consumer-tech post-profitability.

*Sources: [[Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/news/zepto-plans-to-float-rs-11000-crore-ipo-in-july-126052100806_1.html)]([Business Standard](https://www.business-standard.com/companies/news/zepto-plans-to-float-rs-11000-crore-ipo-in-july-126052100806_1.html)) · [[Kotak Neo](https://www.kotakneo.com/news/market-news/sebi-approves-zepto-ipo-2026-update/)]([Kotak Neo](https://www.kotakneo.com/news/market-news/sebi-approves-zepto-ipo-2026-update/)) · [[StartupTalky](https://startuptalky.com/news/zepto-plans-inr-11-000-crore-ipo/)]([StartupTalky](https://startuptalky.com/news/zepto-plans-inr-11-000-crore-ipo/))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### MU (NASDAQ): +19.3% in a single session as UBS triple-targets the stock to $1,625, sending Micron past a $1 trillion market cap for the first time

Micron Technology (MU) closed at $902.88 on May 26, up 19.3% on the day, with an intraday range of $794.60 to $916.76, on volume that was multiples of its average as retail and institutional flows piled in simultaneously. The catalyst was a note from UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri, who tripled his price target from $535 to $1,625 — the most aggressive call on Wall Street — and re-framed Micron as an "AI-native infrastructure giant" rather than a cyclical memory maker. Arcuri's thesis rests on two structural arguments: long-term supply agreements (LTAs) now covering 60-70% of industry server DDR5 volumes at near-spot pricing, and Micron's entire 2026 HBM4 capacity already sold out under those same multi-year contracts. The market cap crossed $1.01 trillion on the print, making Micron the latest name to join NVDA, AVGO, TSM, and Samsung in the exclusive trillion-dollar semiconductor tier.

**Why it matters.** The LTA argument is the key structural shift here: if 60-70% of DDR5 volumes are now locked at partially fixed pricing, the brutal quarter-to-quarter spot-market swings that have historically destroyed memory earnings visibility are materially dampened. That is the same "Nvidia-like earnings predictability" argument that re-rated NVDA's multiple in 2023, and Arcuri is explicitly applying the same framework to MU through C2029. For the AI engineer watching the semis complex, HBM4 is the memory bottleneck inside every next-generation GPU cluster — Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung are the only three companies that can produce it at scale, and confirmed sell-outs through year-end mean supply is not catching up to demand. The read-across to Indian IT and semiconductor assembly names (like HCLTECH, which services hyperscaler infra) is that AI memory procurement is now a multi-year contracted cycle, not a spot-market opportunity.

**Action.** Micron's most recent SEC filing is its 10-Q for the quarter ended February 27, 2025 (filed April 2025); the next quarterly report covering HBM4 volume disclosures and LTA contract details will be the Q2 FY2026 10-Q. The Arcuri note priced the stock at approximately 15x his C2029E EPS of $117 on a one-year discounted basis — the key variable to track is whether actual LTA contract disclosures in the next earnings call (due in late June) confirm the 60-70% DDR5 lock-in figure. Watch $876 (prior 52-week high) as the level that now becomes support on any consolidation.

*Sources: [cnbc.com](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/05/26/micron-stock-trillion-market-cap.html) · [sherwood.news](https://sherwood.news/markets/micron-soars-ubs-more-than-triples-price-target-to-1625-wall-street-high-memory-chips-ai-boom/) · [thetechmarketer.com](https://thetechmarketer.com/micron-mu-stock-2026-ubs-1625-target-trillion-hbm/)*

---

### MRVL (NASDAQ): +5.7% on May 26 ahead of Q1 FY27 earnings after the close today, options market pricing an 11.8% binary reaction as custom-ASIC thesis faces its first major test of 2026

Marvell Technology (MRVL) gained 5.7% on May 26 to approximately $196, extending a year-to-date gain of roughly 100% as Wall Street loaded up ahead of Q1 FY27 results due after the close on May 27. Street consensus sits at $2.40 billion in revenue, implying 27% year-over-year growth, and $0.80 in non-GAAP EPS, implying 29% year-over-year growth; options markets are pricing a post-earnings move of plus or minus 11.8% — or approximately $24 per share — reflecting genuine uncertainty about whether the bullish AI ASIC narrative can sustain the stock's doubled-in-a-year valuation. Stifel analyst Tore Svanberg raised his price target from $140 to $210 on May 22, and multiple other institutions including Citigroup and Wells Fargo have clustered targets around the $200-$220 band ahead of the print.

**Why it matters.** Marvell's quarter is the read-across for the entire custom-silicon ecosystem: the company designs bespoke AI accelerators (XPUs) for Amazon's Trainium and Google's custom TPU supply chain, and its optical interconnect business — the fiber that moves data between GPU nodes at multi-terabit speeds — is the other critical bottleneck inside hyperscale AI clusters alongside HBM memory. If MRVL's Q1 revenue prints in-line or above $2.40 billion and management reaffirms or raises its FY27 guidance toward $11 billion, it confirms that hyperscaler AI CapEx is still accelerating in 2026 rather than plateauing. A miss or a cautious Q2 guide would be the first hard data point suggesting that some portion of the AI infrastructure spend is being paused or re-phased, which would send reverberations across NVDA, AVGO, and TSM. For an AI engineer: Marvell's interconnect silicon is what makes 100,000-GPU training clusters physically possible; a booking acceleration signal from management on the call would be a direct signal that the next generation of frontier model infrastructure is being built out now.

**Action.** Marvell's Q4 FY2026 8-K (filed late February 2026) and the associated earnings call transcript contain the most recent disclosure of data center revenue breakdown and custom ASIC design-win pipeline. The critical number to watch on tonight's call is not the Q1 revenue beat/miss alone, but management's qualitative comments on the pace of new hyperscaler design wins and any mention of HBM tie-in demand. The options-implied move of $24 sets a binary range of approximately $172 to $220; the stock has held the $170 level as support throughout its 2026 rally.

*Sources: [moneymorning.com](https://moneymorning.com/2026/05/25/marvell-technology-mrvl-stock-earnings-may-2026) · [tradingkey.com](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-stocks/261928280-marvell-earnings-wall-street-lifts-price-targets-nvidia-amd-tradingkey) · [eciks.org](https://eciks.org/5635-73478-mrvl-stock-reports-earnings-today-after-doubling-in-2026)*

## 🩹 Health & Science

### Leucine Blocks Protein Degradation to Supercharge Mitochondrial Energy Production

Researchers at the University of Cologne discovered that leucine, an essential amino acid abundant in protein-rich foods, suppresses ubiquitin-dependent degradation of outer mitochondrial membrane proteins. The finding, published in Nature Cell Biology in May 2026, was demonstrated in both C. elegans and human lung cancer cells. By preventing the breakdown of key energy-importing proteins on the mitochondrial surface, leucine expands the mitochondrial proteome and enhances metabolic respiration capacity.

**Why it matters.** This establishes a direct mechanistic link between dietary protein intake and cellular energy output, with implications for aging and metabolic disease. Leucine acts as a nutrient signal that adapts mitochondrial function, opening a non-pharmaceutical intervention pathway for combating mitochondrial decline associated with aging. For cancer and metabolic disease researchers, this identifies a targetable node—the SEL1L protein pathway—that could be modulated therapeutically or through precision nutrition strategies.

**Action.** Read the full study "Leucine inhibits degradation of outer mitochondrial membrane proteins to adapt mitochondrial respiration" in Nature Cell Biology (2026). For practitioners: consider leucine-enriched protein intake (chicken, dairy, legumes, fish) as a preventive metabolic intervention. For researchers and biotech founders: the SEL1L pathway represents a validation point for small molecules that could mimic or enhance leucine's stabilization effect on OMM proteins in disease contexts where mitochondrial function is compromised.

*Sources: [[ScienceDaily: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260520233221.htm)]([ScienceDaily: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260520233221.htm)) · [[Nature Cell Biology: Leucine inhibits degradation of outer mitochondrial membrane proteins](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41556-025-01799-3)]([Nature Cell Biology: Leucine inhibits degradation of outer mitochondrial membrane proteins](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41556-025-01799-3)) · [[EurekAlert: From food to fuel: How leucine enhances mitochondrial energy production](https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1104146)]([EurekAlert: From food to fuel: How leucine enhances mitochondrial energy production](https://www.eurekalert.org/news-releases/1104146))*

---

### HELZ2 Protein Identified as Master Regulator of Liver Cholesterol Release

UT Southwestern researchers identified HELZ2 as a molecular gatekeeper controlling apoB production, the structural protein required for cholesterol-carrying particles that accumulate in arterial plaques. The study, published in Circulation in May 2026, shows HELZ2 suppresses the genetic instructions needed to produce apoB. By modulating this single regulatory node, researchers demonstrated control over the amount of cholesterol the liver releases into circulation.

**Why it matters.** Current cholesterol-lowering drugs target downstream processing or uptake; HELZ2 represents an upstream regulatory lever on production itself. This is a therapeutically relevant discovery for cardiovascular disease prevention, with potential applications in both familial hypercholesterolemia and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. For biotech investors and drug developers, this identifies a new target class and validates the commercial opportunity in hepatic metabolic control.

**Action.** Read the UT Southwestern press release and the Circulation publication. For drug developers: HELZ2 inhibition or activation could become a new mechanism class for cholesterol management, potentially addressing patients who don't respond adequately to statins or other LDL-lowering agents. Clinical translation would likely target familial hypercholesterolemia first, where upstream control offers clear advantage over current therapies.

*Sources: [[ScienceDaily: Scientists discover hidden liver switch that cuts harmful cholesterol](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260523103947.htm)]([ScienceDaily: Scientists discover hidden liver switch that cuts harmful cholesterol](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/05/260523103947.htm)) · [[UT Southwestern: Study identifies key protein regulating cholesterol release](https://www.utsouthwestern.edu/newsroom/articles/year-2026/feb-key-protein-cholesterol-release.html)]([UT Southwestern: Study identifies key protein regulating cholesterol release](https://www.utsouthwestern.edu/newsroom/articles/year-2026/feb-key-protein-cholesterol-release.html)) · [[Mirage News: Scientists Find Liver Switch That Cuts Bad Cholesterol](https://www.miragenews.com/scientists-find-liver-switch-that-cuts-bad-1679678/)]([Mirage News: Scientists Find Liver Switch That Cuts Bad Cholesterol](https://www.miragenews.com/scientists-find-liver-switch-that-cuts-bad-1679678/))*
